title: galium aparine subsp. aparinella
intro: Galium aparine subsp. aparinella, also known as stickyweed or catchweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the bedstraw family (Rubiaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Galium aparine subsp. aparinella include relief from joint pain, reduction of fever, and treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of urinary tract infections. Herbal preparations of Galium aparine subsp. aparinella may be consumed as a tea, decoction, or tincture, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions or side effects.
